IZKVerifier","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"author":"ZeroID Cryptography Team","details":"An RSA accumulator is a single group element V that commits to a set S:        V = g^{\u220f_{s\u2208S} H(s)} mod N      where N is an RSA modulus, g is a generator of QR_N, and H maps members      to primes. Non-membership witnesses prove element e \u2209 S using B\u00e9zout's identity:        Given GCD(H(e), \u220f H(s)) = 1, there exist a, b such that        a\u00b7H(e) + b\u00b7\u220fH(s) = 1  \u2192  d^{H(e)} \u00b7 V^b = g  (non-membership witness d)      Accumulator updates (revocations) are verified on-chain via Wesolowski proofs      of exponentiation, delegated to an external IExponentiationVerifier that uses      the EVM MODEXP precompile (0x05).      Non-membership proofs are verified via an external IZKVerifier (Groth16) that      uses the BN254 pairing precompile (0x08).","errors":{"AccessControlBadConfirmation()":[{"details":"The caller of a function is not the expected one. NOTE: Don't confuse with {AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount}."}],"AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount(address,bytes32)":[{"details":"The `account` is missing a role."}],"EnforcedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is paused."}],"ExpectedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is not paused."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}]},"events":{"Paused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is triggered by `account`."},"RoleAdminChanged(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32)":{"details":"Emitted when `newAdminRole` is set as ``role``'s admin role, replacing `previousAdminRole` `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is the starting admin for all roles, despite {RoleAdminChanged} not being emitted to signal this."},"RoleGranted(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is granted `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call. This account bears the admin role (for the granted role). Expected in cases where the role was granted using the internal {AccessControl-_grantRole}."},"RoleRevoked(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is revoked `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call:   - if using `revokeRole`, it is the admin role bearer   - if using `renounceRole`, it is the role bearer (i.e. `account`)"},"Unpaused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"batchRevoke(bytes32,(bytes32[],bytes,bytes,uint256))":{"details":"More gas-efficient than individual revocations. The new accumulator value      is V' = V^{\u220f H(cred_i)} mod N.","params":{"accId":"Accumulator identifier","batch":"Batch update containing credentials and proof"}},"getRoleAdmin(bytes32)":{"details":"Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}."},"grantRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event."},"hasRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Returns `true` if `account` has been granted `role`."},"initializeAccumulator(bytes32,bytes)":{"params":{"accId":"Unique identifier","initialValue":"Initial accumulator value (typically the generator g)"}},"linkRegistry(bytes32,address)":{"params":{"accId":"The accumulator","registry":"Address of the credential registry"}},"paused()":{"details":"Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise."},"publishWitnessUpdate(bytes32,(uint256,uint256,bytes,bytes,bytes))":{"params":{"accId":"Accumulator identifier","delta":"The witness update delta"}},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokeCredential(bytes32,bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"params":{"accId":"Accumulator identifier","credentialHash":"Hash of the credential to revoke","newValue":"New accumulator value: V' = V^{H(credential)} mod N","proof":"ABI-encoded Wesolowski quotient Q (modulus-sized bytes)"}},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"setExponentiationVerifier(address)":{"params":{"verifier":"Address of a contract implementing IExponentiationVerifier"}},"setParameters(bytes,bytes,uint256)":{"params":{"generator":"A generator g of the quadratic residues mod N","modulusBitLength":"Bit length of the modulus","rsaModulus":"The RSA modulus N (must be \u2265 2048 bits)"}},"setZKVerifier(address,bytes32)":{"params":{"circuitId":"Circuit ID for the non-membership proof circuit","verifier":"Address of a contract implementing IZKVerifier"}},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas."},"verifyNonMembership(bytes32,(bytes,bytes,bytes32,uint256),bytes)":{"details":"Delegates to the external IZKVerifier for Groth16 proof verification.      The ZK circuit proves knowledge of a B\u00e9zout non-membership witness      (d, b) such that d^{H(e)} \u00b7 V^b \u2261 g (mod N), without revealing d or b.","params":{"accId":"Accumulator identifier","witness":"Non-membership witness (used for public input derivation)","zkProof":"ABI-encoded Groth16Proof"},"returns":{"valid":"  True if the credential is confirmed non-revoked"}},"verifyNonMembershipAtEpoch(bytes32,(bytes,bytes,bytes32,uint256),uint256,bytes)":{"params":{"accId":"Accumulator identifier","historicalEpoch":"The epoch to verify against","witness":"Non-membership witness","zkProof":"ABI-encoded Groth16Proof"},"returns":{"valid":"          True if non-revoked at the given epoch"}}},"stateVariables":{"MAX_BATCH_SIZE":{"details":"Empirical gas profile (2048-bit RSA modulus, 20-round Miller-Rabin):      Adversarial worst-case: credential hashes requiring ~580 iterations      of the _hashToPrime search consume ~8.5M gas (single credential).      Marginal cost per additional worst-case credential: ~7.8M gas.      At 30M block gas limit with 20% safety margin (24M budget):        MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 1 + floor((24M - 8.5M) / 7.8M) = 2      Figures are from adversarial regression benchmarks with fresh-deploy      isolation. Warm-slot discount from same-tx setup is ~16K gas      (<0.1% of measured values), accounted for in the test assertion.      Larger batches should be split into multiple transactions."}},"title":"AccumulatorRevocation","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"HASH_TO_PRIME_DOMAIN()":{"notice":"Domain separator for hashing credentials to primes"},"NON_MEMBERSHIP_CIRCUIT_ID()":{"notice":"Circuit ID for the non-membership ZK proof circuit"},"batchRevoke(bytes32,(bytes32[],bytes,bytes,uint256))":{"notice":"Batch revoke multiple credentials in a single transaction."},"exponentiationVerifier()":{"notice":"External Wesolowski proof verifier (uses MODEXP precompile)"},"getCurrentState(bytes32)":{"notice":"Get the current accumulator value hash and epoch"},"getParameters()":{"notice":"Get accumulator parameters"},"getSnapshot(bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Get a historical snapshot"},"getWitnessUpdateDelta(bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Get the witness update delta for an epoch"},"initializeAccumulator(bytes32,bytes)":{"notice":"Initialize a new accumulator instance."},"isRevoked(bytes32,bytes32)":{"notice":"Check if a credential is explicitly revoked"},"linkRegistry(bytes32,address)":{"notice":"Link an accumulator to a credential registry contract."},"nonMembershipCircuitId()":{"notice":"Circuit ID for non-membership proofs (configurable)"},"publishWitnessUpdate(bytes32,(uint256,uint256,bytes,bytes,bytes))":{"notice":"Publish a witness update delta for holders."},"revokeCredential(bytes32,bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"Revoke a single credential by adding it to the accumulator."},"setExponentiationVerifier(address)":{"notice":"Set the external Wesolowski exponentiation verifier."},"setParameters(bytes,bytes,uint256)":{"notice":"Set the RSA accumulator parameters (one-time setup)."},"setZKVerifier(address,bytes32)":{"notice":"Set the external Groth16 ZK verifier for non-membership proofs."},"totalRevocations()":{"notice":"Total revocations across all accumulators"},"verifyNonMembership(bytes32,(bytes,bytes,bytes32,uint256),bytes)":{"notice":"Verify that a credential is NOT in the revocation accumulator."},"verifyNonMembershipAtEpoch(bytes32,(bytes,bytes,bytes32,uint256),uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Verify non-membership at a historical epoch (point-in-time check)."},"zkVerifier()":{"notice":"External Groth16 ZK proof verifier (uses BN254 pairing precompile)"}},"notice":"Privacy-preserving credential revocation using RSA-style cryptographic         accumulators. batchRevoke(bytes32,(bytes32[],bytes,byt.details: More gas-efficient than individual revocations. The new accumulator value      is V' = V^{∏ H(cred_i)} mod N.

params.batch: Batch update containing credentials and proof

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

params.initialValue: Initial accumulator value (typically the generator g)

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

params.credentialHash: Hash of the credential to revoke
params.newValue: New accumulator value: V' = V^{H(credential)} mod N

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

params.verifier: Address of a contract implementing IExponentiationVerifier

params.generator: A generator g of the quadratic residues mod N
params.rsaModulus: The RSA modulus N (must be ≥ 2048 bits)

params.circuitId: Circuit ID for the non-membership proof circuit
params.verifier: Address of a contract implementing IZKVerifier

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

verifyNonMembership(bytes32,(bytes,bytes.details: Delegates to the external IZKVerifier for Groth16 proof verification.      The ZK circuit proves knowledge of a Bézout non-membership witness      (d, b) such that d^{H(e)} · V^b ≡ g (mod N), without revealing d or b.

params.witness: Non-membership witness (used for public input derivation)

returns.valid: True if the credential is confirmed non-revoked
True if non-revoked at the given epoch

HASH_TO_PRIME_DOMAIN().notice: Domain separator for hashing credentials to primes

NON_MEMBERSHIP_CIRCUIT_ID().notice: Circuit ID for the non-membership ZK proof circuit

batchRevoke(bytes32,(bytes32[],bytes,byt.notice: Batch revoke multiple credentials in a single transaction.

exponentiationVerifier().notice: External Wesolowski proof verifier (uses MODEXP precompile)

getCurrentState(bytes32).notice: Get the current accumulator value hash and epoch

getWitnessUpdateDelta(bytes32,uint256).notice: Get the witness update delta for an epoch

isRevoked(bytes32,bytes32).notice: Check if a credential is explicitly revoked

linkRegistry(bytes32,address).notice: Link an accumulator to a credential registry contract.

publishWitnessUpdate(bytes32,(uint256,ui.notice: Publish a witness update delta for holders.

revokeCredential(bytes32,bytes32,bytes,b.notice: Revoke a single credential by adding it to the accumulator.

setExponentiationVerifier(address).notice: Set the external Wesolowski exponentiation verifier.

setParameters(bytes,bytes,uint256).notice: Set the RSA accumulator parameters (one-time setup).

setZKVerifier(address,bytes32).notice: Set the external Groth16 ZK verifier for non-membership proofs.

verifyNonMembership(bytes32,(bytes,bytes.notice: Verify that a credential is NOT in the revocation accumulator.

verifyNonMembershipAtEpoch(bytes32,(byte.notice: Verify non-membership at a historical epoch (point-in-time check).

zkVerifier().notice: External Groth16 ZK proof verifier (uses BN254 pairing precompile)
